selectionRules: prefer boolean rows with simple validators and prior temp-config proof
avoid anything that could make recovery depend on mouse, cursor, pointer focus, workspace focus, or normal pointer behavior

rows.excludedReason: excluded because these rows can hide/remove cursor visibility; recovery cannot depend on visible cursor or pointer behavior
proofRequiredBeforeEnablement: proof that recovery does not depend on mouse input
proof that recovery does not depend on visible cursor
proof that recovery does not depend on app UI
proof that recovery does not depend on Hyprland keybinds
proof that recovery does not depend on pointer focus
proof that recovery does not depend on workspace focus
proof that recovery does not depend on normal pointer behavior

selectionReason: After enabling cursor.sync_gsettings_theme, the remaining 21 cursor/input rows are all cursor visibility, hardware cursor, warping, zoom, or monitor-targeting critical. No remaining row is a low cursor policy row, and selecting any of them now would violate the requirement that recovery remain fully independent of cursor, mouse, pointer focus, workspace focus, and normal pointer behavior.
nextRequiredWork: prove a stronger input-independent live recovery boundary before considering visibility, hardware cursor, warping, zoom, or monitor-targeting rows
prefer a dedicated design sprint for one risk class at a time instead of selecting a mixed cursor/input subset
keep all remaining cursor/input rows blocked until separately approved
